title.value: tert-Butyldimethylsilyl-Directed Highly Enantioselective Approach to Axially Chiral α-Allenols

abstract.value: A highly efficient and enantioselective synthesis of axially chiral α-allenols was realized in practical yields with 96–99 % ee or de from TBS-protected propargylic alcohols, aldehydes, and a commercially available, inexpensive, chiral, secondary amine (S)-α,α-diphenylprolinol or its enantiomer followed by desilylation. The easily removable TBS group not only acts as a protecting group, but also as a possible sterically directing group for the excellent enantioselectivity and in situ prevention of possible allene racemization.

warnings: Publication year is supported as 2013 by journal footer/front matter, despite the path hint and initial metadata suggesting 2012.
Path slug suggests 2012, but the article front matter and journal citation support publication year 2013.
The reaction type and catalyst/method labels are approximate rule matches for a ZnBr2/chiral amine-mediated aldehyde coupling that proceeds through a propargylic amine intermediate to allenols.

value.ligand_or_chiral_source: prolinol-derived and other chiral amine catalysts
